days.title: Delhi to Jibhi | Overnight Journey
activities: You’ll assemble at the pickup point.
Board the vehicle and start your overnight journey to Jibhi.
Start your adventurous trip to Spiti.

days.title: Jibhi Arrival | Day at Leisure
activities: Check into your hotel, freshen up, and relax after the long journey.
Enjoy a delicious dinner and a comfortable night’s stay.

days.title: Jibhi to Chitkul
activities: Wake up to the sound of chirping birds and enjoy a hearty breakfast.
Begin your journey towards Chitkul, India’s last village near the Tibet border.
Cross the beautiful Jalori Pass and witness changing landscapes through Kinnaur Valley.
Stop by the famous Kinnaur Gate for photos.
On arrival in Chitkul, check into your stay, have dinner, and rest.
Highlights of the Day: Chitkul, Kinnaur Gate

days.title: Chitkul to Kalpa
activities: Start your day with breakfast and head towards Kalpa.
Make a stop at Reckong Peo town for stunning views of Kinner Kailash Peak (weather permitting).
Visit the 3000-year-old Kalpa Monastery and the Suicide Point viewpoint.
Check into your hotel, have dinner, and spend the night in Kalpa.
Highlights of the Day: Kinner Kailash views, ancient monastery, Reckong Peo stopover.

days.title: Kalpa to Dhankar
activities: Have an early breakfast and start your journey towards Dhankar, the former capital of Spiti Valley.
Halt at Khab to see the confluence of Sutlej and Spiti rivers.
Pass through Nako village and pay respects at Tabo Monastery.
Check into your stay in Dhankar and enjoy dinner.
Attend an informative session about Dhankar’s culture and history with a local guide.
Highlights of the Day: Khab confluence, Nako village, Tabo Monastery visit.

days.title: Dhankar to Kaza
activities: Begin your day with breakfast and explore Dhankar Monastery and Dhankar Fort for incredible valley views.
Drive to Kaza, stopping at Kungri Monastery in Pin Valley along the way.
Check into your stay in Kaza and enjoy some leisure time exploring local cafes and the market.
Have dinner and spend the night in Kaza.
Highlights of the Day: Dhankar Monastery, Dhankar Fort, Kungri Monastery.

days.title: Spiti Sightseeing Day
activities: After breakfast, visit Key Monastery, the largest monastery in Spiti Valley.
Experience Asia’s highest suspension bridge at Chicham Bridge.
Post a letter from the world’s highest post office in Hikkim.
Drive to Komic, one of the highest motorable villages in the world.
End the day at Langza, the land of fossils, and witness the iconic Buddha statue.
Return to Kaza for dinner and overnight stay.
Highlights of the Day: Key Monastery, Hikkim post office, Langza fossil village.

days.title: Kaza to Chandratal
activities: Enjoy breakfast and set off towards the crescent-shaped Chandratal Lake.
Cross the iconic Kunzum Pass with stunning Himalayan views.
Reach your campsite near Chandratal and settle in.
Spend some peaceful time by the lakeside, soaking in the natural beauty.
Dinner and overnight stay in camps under a starry sky.
*Note: In case of bad weather, alternate stay will be arranged in Batal/Manali/Kaza.
Highlights of the Day: Kunzum Pass drive, Chandratal Lake.

days.title: Chandratal to Manali | Overnight Journey to Delhi
activities: Have breakfast and check out from your campsite.
Travel through Atal Tunnel on your way back to Manali.
Stop at the famous Chacha-Chachi Ka Dhaba for refreshments.
Board your overnight bus from Manali to Delhi.
Highlights of the Day: Scenic return journey, Atal Tunnel, Chacha-Chachi Ka Dhaba

days.title: Delhi Arrival | Tour Ends
activities: Arrive in Delhi in the morning with wonderful memories of your trip.

days.title: Delhi to Shimla | Overnight Journey
activities: Get Ready for the adventurous journey to Spiti Valley.
Pack your winter layers and excitement as your dream Himalayan journey begins.
Get comfy as you hop aboard your overnight bus and begin the journey to Shimla.

days.title: Shimla to Chitkul/Sangla (225 Kms)
activities: You’ll arrive in Shimla early morning via the scenic Hindustan-Tibet Highway.
Freshen up at a designated stop (about 2 hours after Shimla) before switching to your Tempo Traveler for the rest of the journey.
As you drive deeper into the mountains, you'll feel the magic of Kinnaur Valley.
On arrival at Chitkul or Sangla, check into your cozy stay.
End your day with a warm dinner and settle in for the night.
Highlights of the Day: Kinnaur Valley

days.title: Chitkul/Sangla to Kalpa (50 Kms)
activities: Enjoy a hearty breakfast before you hit the road to Kalpa, a stunning Himalayan village.
Along the way, you’ll stop at Reckong Peo, grab some snacks, take in the views, or click a few photos.
If time allows, explore the historic Kamru Fort en route.
Once in Kalpa, visit the ancient Kalpa Monastery and take a walk to the dramatic Suicide Point, the views of Kinner Kailash will take your breath away.
After check-in, enjoy your dinner and call it a night.
Highlights of the Day: Kalpa, Reckong Peo, Kamru Fort, Kalpa Monastery, Suicide Point

days.title: Kalpa to Nako (100 Kms)
activities: Post breakfast, continue your road trip to Nako, a beautiful hamlet tucked in the trans-Himalayas.
Make a stop at Khab, where the Sutlej and Spiti rivers meet, take in the dramatic landscape.
Once in Nako, stroll around the peaceful Nako Lake, perfect for quiet reflection.
Visit the spiritual Nako Monastery and learn about local Tibetan traditions.
Settle into your basic accommodation, enjoy a simple dinner, and rest up. Highlights of the Day: Khab sangam, Nako Lake, Nako Monastery

days.title: Nako to Kaza (110 Kms)
activities: Start your day with breakfast and then drive to Kaza, the heart of Spiti Valley.
Visit the ancient Tabo Monastery, over 1000 years old, and admire its murals and frescoes.
Next, explore the cliffside Dhankar Monastery and climb up to Dhankar Fort for jaw-dropping valley views.
You’ll reach Kaza by evening.
Check into your stay and take some time to explore the town on foot.
Enjoy a warm dinner and relax under a sky full of stars.
Highlights of the Day: Kaza, Tabo Monastery, Dhankar Monastery, Dhankar Fort

days.title: Spiti Sightseeing Day (Komic, Hikkim, Langza, Key, Chicham)
activities: After breakfast, get ready for an epic day of Spiti sightseeing.
Start at the peaceful Key Monastery, you might even get to sit with monks during morning prayer.
Walk across Chicham Bridge, Asia’s highest, and peer into the gorge below.
Post a letter from Hikkim, home to the world’s highest post office - a great keepsake!
Visit Komic, one of the world’s highest motorable villages.
End your day at Langza, where you’ll see the giant Buddha statue watching over the valley and history buffs can explore museum.
Return to Kaza for dinner and a well-earned rest.
Highlights of the Day: Key monastery, Chicham Bridge, Hikkim, Langza, Komic

days.title: Kaza to Kalpa (200 Kms)
activities: Say goodbye to Kaza after breakfast and begin the drive back to Kalpa.
On the way, if time and weather allow, stop at the majestic Lingti Falls, frozen and magical during winter.
Reach Kalpa by evening, enjoy a delicious dinner, and tuck in for the night.
Highlights of the Day: Lingti falls

days.title: Kalpa to Shimla (222 Kms)
activities: After breakfast, start your journey back to Shimla.
Make a quick photo stop at the Rock Tunnel, famously known as the Gateway to Kinnaur — it’s Insta-worthy!
Once you reach Shimla, board your overnight bus back to Delhi.
Highlights of the Day: Rock Tunnel

days.title: Delhi Arrival | Tour Ends
activities: You’ll arrive in Delhi early morning, bringing your incredible Spiti journey to a close.
Say your goodbyes, exchange contacts, and take home memories that’ll last a lifetime.

days.title: Arrival at Leh Airport + Acclimatization Only
activities: Pick up from Leh Airport and proceeding for Hotel Check-in. Since you have arrived by air, it is strictly advised to relax & acclimatize with Leh altitude & weather today.
Take full day rest & gear up for upcoming journeys.
Dinner and overnight stay in Leh.

days.title: Leh City Tour
activities: Start towards Sham Valley in the morning after Breakfast- visiting Hall of fame, Spituk Monastery, the confluence of Indus & Zanskar River (Sangam), Gurudwara Sri Pathar Sahib, Magnetic Hill.
Return to Leh by evening. Have dinner and overnight stay in Leh.
*Consult our Tour Planner to add River Rafting adventure to this Trip.
days.highlight: Hall of fame, Spituk Monastery, confluence of Indus and Zanskar river, Gurudwara Sri Pathar Sahib, Magnetic Hill.

days.title: Leh to Nubra
activities: After Breakfast, we travel towards one of the Highest Motorable Road of The World, Khardung La Pass. Have a cup of Tea there, get yourself clicked with the Khardungla Signboard by BRO & proceed towards Nubra valley covering Diskit Monastery enroute.

Check-in at Hotel/Camp, freshen up & later you can have a Double Hump Bactarian Camel ride. Don't forget to click this unique mammal, found in the cold Desert. It was once used to carry the trade material on the silk route.
After dinner Overnight at Hunter/Nubra Camps/Hotel.
days.highlight: Double Hump Bactarian Camel ride, Deskit Monastery

days.title: Nubra to Pangong (250 kms)
activities: After Breakfast, check out from the camps & visit Diskit Monastery. If time permits you can also go for ATV ride (personal expense) and then continue further till Pangong via Shyok Valley.

Pangong Lake is one of the most beautiful & scenic high altitude saltwater lakes. The beauty of this place is such that various Bollywood movies have been shot around this lake. Another fact about the lake is that one-third of this lake is controlled by India & rest two-third lies under Chinese province. Check In to the campsite and later go for a stroll around Pangong Lake in the evening.
Overnight at camps in Pangong.
days.highlight: ATV ride (personal expense), Shyok Valley, Pangong lake

days.title: Pangong to TsoMoriri
activities: After Breakfast, check out from the stay after that proceed for Tso Moriri Lake.
Tso Moriri is another high altitude lake in the Ladakh region and comes under Tso Moriri wetland sanctuary
Mighty mountains, snow-capped peaks, lush green fields and the deep blue water stretching to the horizon, Tso Moriri offers it all.
Have dinner and Overnight stay at Guesthouse in Tso-Moriri.

days.title: Tsomoriri to Leh
activities: After Breakfast, check out from Camps for the return journey to Leh. Enroute take a short halt at Changla Pass and visit Hemis Monastery & 3-Idiots School.
Return to Leh in the evening.
Dinner and overnight stay at Leh.

days.title: Leh Sightseeing & Rest Day
activities: Have your breakfast with beautiful views of Leh. Today, We will cover remaining sightseeing of Leh.

Firstly you will go for peaceful Thiksey Monastery afterwards we will move towards the former palace of the royal family of Leh - Leh Palace, Ladakh famous - Shanti Stupa and buy some souvenirs from Leh Market.
In the evening come back to your stay for dinner.
Overnight stay in your hotel.
days.highlight: Thiksey Monastery, Leh Palace, Shanti stupa

days.title: Leh to Jispa
activities: Start early Morning after Breakfast, head towards Jispa. It will take 9 hours to reach Jispa. You will pass through Zing Zing bar and Sarchu. Later on continue to Jispa.
Jispa is located on 10,500 feet above the sea level. It's a little and calm village. You can buy good hand made handicraft items in jispa local market.
Late evening you will reach to Jispa. On arrival do check-in formalities and enjoy dinner in your campsite with beautiful view of Bhaga river.
Overnight stay at Jispa.
days.highlight: Zing Zing bar, local market exploration at Jispa, Bhaga river

days.title: Jispa to Kaza via Chatru
activities: After early breakfast you will transfer to Kaza . You will cross Keylong, Tandi bridge, Pagal Nallah, Khoksar, Chatru and Gramphu en route.

Kaza is the biggest and well developed town in Spiti valley. Located in the Trans-Himalayan region of India at an elevation of 3,800 meters. It is surrounded by high mountains, crystal clear river and barren landscapes intermix with green landscapes and meadows.
In evening you will reach Kaza. Do check-in formalities and have some rest. In free time you can roam around Your hotel and can explore local market of Kaza. Have dinner overnight stay in your hotel.

days.title: Kaza (Langza - Komic - Hikkim)
activities: Start our day after breakfast and start sightseeing Our first stop will be Langza (Fossil Village), famous for the big Buddha Statue & Fossils of marine creatures and plants, visit en route Komic and 18 km from Kaza. Spend some time at Komic, the highest inhabited village in the world. Get clicking with the locals and spend some time at the monastery.  Later start your journey to Hikkim, the highest post office in the world, which is about 5 km from Komic. Send postcards to your near and dear ones. In evening head back to Kaza.
Dinner and Night stay at Kaza.

days.title: Kaza (Dhankar via Pin Valley - Tabo - Mud - Gue)
activities: After breakfast we'll be visiting Kungri Monastery in Pin valley and then head towards Mud Village. We'll also be visiting 1021 years old Tabo Monastery, which is a UNESCO World Heritage Site. After Lunch, we'll be going to Gue Village, which houses a 500 years old mummy. We'll be ending our day at Dhankar and visiting Dhankar Monastery. In evening head back to Kaza.
Dinner and overnight stay at Kaza.
days.highlight: Kungri Monastery, Gue Village, Dhankar Monastery,

days.title: Kaza to Chandrataal Lake via Kibber - Kee Monastery and Chicham
activities: Head towards one of the most scenic lakes in India, i.e., Chandra Tal also called as Moon Lake via Kunzum Pass and Losar. The lake is beautiful, surrounded by mountains and is situated at an altitude of 4300 meters. Enjoy the views, get yourself clicked with the lake and spend some leisure time. En Route we'll be stopping at Kee Monastery which is the largest monastery in the entire Spiti Valley. We'll also be visiting Kibber, which is one of the highest motorable village in the world and Chicham Khab Bridge which is the highest bridge in Asia at a height of 13,500 + fts.
Return to the campsite before sunset and if the skies are clear, wait for an amazing sunset on CB12 (Chandra Bhagha 12).
Dinner and overnight stay in camps at Chandra Taal.
days.highlight: Chandra Tal , Kee Monastery, Chicham bridge, CB12 (Chandra Bhagha 12)

days.title: Chandrataal to Manali
activities: Today is the last day of our tour and we will start for Manali early in the morning after breakfast.

You are already familiar with the route it's the same we took on day two, crossing Bada Darra, Chota Darra and Rohtang Pass to finally reach Manali by late afternoon. On arrival done check - in formalities and have some rest. You can experience night life of Mall Road in evening, come back to your hotel for dinner. Overnight stay in hotel.

days.title: Manali Departure
activities: You will depart this day with lot's of memories of Himachal and Ladakh.
Enjoy your breakfast and check out from the hotel.
And viola! Tour ends here.

days.title: Sarahan to Chitkul via Sangla Valley
activities: After breakfast head towards Chitkul enroute you will pass through the Sangla Valley. we will be traveling from Sarahan to Chitkul. Chitkul is 106 km from Sarahan and is a small beautiful village that lies near the border and India and Tibet. Chitkul is the last inhabited village in this region.

from today onwards we will cross Karcham Wangtoo Dam, A scene view of confluence of Baspa with Satlaj River, Kamroo Sangla Valley Gate, Baspa Bridge and Kamru Fort before finally reaching Chitkul in the evening. We will go for a stroll to the riverside later in the evening
Dinner and overnight stay at Chitkul

days.title: Chitkul to Kalpa Transfer
activities: Post breakfast, we will take you to the ITBP check post & Akhri Dhaba (enjoy lunch in India's Last Dhaba on your own expense)

After that we’ll start our journey of kalpa. We’ll be passing through the popular town Sangla before entering the spectacular Kinnaur Valley. Before entering Kalpa at 7549 feet, you might catch a glimpse of the famous Kinner Kailash Peak – said to be the summer adobe of Lord Shiva while it’s covered in snow. Kalpa Monastery, Suicide Point at Roghi, the beautiful Reckong Peo town are some of the most amazing places to see here.
At Kalpa, you will warm up with a piping cup of tea before checking into your homestay/hotel.
Dinner and Overnight stay at the property in Kalpa.

days.title: Kalpa to Nako Transfer
activities: Post breakfast, we’ll continue our journey on the famous Hindustan – Tibet Highway or the NH 22. The journey today is going to be exciting with treacherous patches on the way and making our stop at Khab, the confluence of Spiti and Sutlej rivers which you will be easily able to differentiate the colour of.

We’ll be visiting the frozen Nako lake, the Nako Monastery and a very old, small, ancient temple said to have the impression of Gautam Buddha’s foot. Spend some time getting mesmerized to one of the most beautiful sunsets you’ll ever see from the sunset point in Nako.
Dinner and Overnight stay at Homestay in Nako.

days.title: Nako to Kaza via Tabo , Gue and Dhankar
activities: We will start this day after breakfast from Nako and visit the 1021-year-old Tabo Monastery, UNESCO World Heritage Site. You can walk around this sacred site and gain a deeper understanding of Buddhism. Unlike other monasteries in Spiti Valley, the Tabo monastery is located at the bottom of the valley. You’ll be able to witness the difference in the structure of the current monasteries from that of the Tabo Monastery which is actually made of mud.
We'll also be visiting Gue Monastery and Dhankar Monastery. After Dhankar, we'll visit Kungri Monastery* and Mudh Village*.
We’ll reach Kaza before evening and check-in into our hotel. The rest of the day will be at leisure. You can go around the Kaza city, visit the Monastery, check out the local food and the markets.
Dinner at the hotel in Kaza.

days.title: Kaza (Langza - Komic - Hikkim)
activities: Start our day after breakfast and start sightseeing Our first stop will be Langza (Fossil Village), famous for the big Buddha Statue & Fossils of marine creatures and plants, visit en route Komic and 18 km from Kaza. Spend some time at Komic, the highest inhabited village in the world. Get clicking with the locals and spend some time at the monastery.  Later start your journey to Hikkim, the highest post office in the world, which is about 5 km from Komic. Send postcards to your near and dear ones. In evening head back to Kaza.
Dinner and Night stay at Kaza.

days.title: Kaza to Chandrataal Lake via Kibber - Kee Monastery and Chicham
activities: Head towards one of the most scenic lakes in India, i.e., Chandra Tal also called as Moon Lake via Kunzum Pass and Losar. The lake is beautiful, surrounded by mountains and is situated at an altitude of 4300 meters. Enjoy the views, get yourself clicked with the lake and spend some leisure time. En Route we'll be stopping at Kee Monastery which is the largest monastery in the entire Spiti Valley. We'll also be visiting Kibber, which is one of the highest motorable village in the world and Chicham Khas Bridge which isthe highest bridge in Asia at a height of 13,500 + fts.
Return to the campsite before sunset and if the skies are clear, wait for an amazing sunset on CB12 ( Chandra Bhagha 12).
Dinner and overnight stay in camps at Chandra Taal.

days.title: Chandrataal to Manali
activities: Today is the last day of our tour and we will start for Manali early in the morning after breakfast. You will be dropped Manali by afternoon.
Tour End's Here...
